For future improvement opportunities, to require the BAC and other concerned offices, to strictly conform to the provisions of RA 9184 and its RIRR in all stages of the procurement of goods and services, and infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Final evaluation of the SAT on the justification submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to:

Discourage the practice of procuring goods and services through reimbursement basis and strictly adhere to Section 93 of Presidential Decree No. 1445, wherein payments should be paid thru checks and directly to the supplier, in compliance to the sound internal control system over disbursement of funds. 

Require the deduction of withholding taxes from all purchases of goods, supplies and materials for remittance to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) in adherence to Item J of Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to observe the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We commended management for institutionalizing a functioning Solid Waste Management Board as required under RA 9003, the Philippine Ecological Solid Waste Management Act.
observations.managementAction: Management is continuously  complying with the requirement of RA 9003.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to send demand letters to delinquent taxpayers and conduct an information campaign to all real property owners within the Municipality, with emphasis of granting discounts, when paid before due dates, and the possibility of losing their property for not paying at all.
observations.managementAction:
The COVID 19 pandemic proved to be a hindrance in the distribution of notices and demand letters.

Continue finding ways to accelerate collection of RPT, with focus on delinquent RPT.

Send notices and demand letters to the delinquent real property owners.

The present COVID 19 pandemic and the ASF epidemic that affected the source of livelihood of consumers contributed to the inability of the same to pay their balances.

Demand letters were already sent.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to:

direct the responsible Office to immediately send demand letters to delinquent concessionaires to pay their arrears, otherwise, disconnection will be imposed;

strictly enforce its policy on the disconnection of water service to delinquent concessionaires and disconnect water service connections of concessionaires with account not paid for three (3) consecutive months; and

ensure that the SMWSS should not be selective but instead be consistent and strict in investigating concessionaires with long overdue accounts that are still connected to date and imposing a sanction on those employees found responsible of violating the policy.
observations.managementAction:
Failure to pay the water bills is due to the COVID 19 Pandemic which hinders the consumers to pay their balances.

Demand letters to be sent to the delinquent consumers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to strictly implement the provision of Section 2A.09 of Market Code of San Mateo that mandates the cancellation of lease contract for lessees who are found to have sub-leased their stalls or to prohibit sub-leasing of market stalls to protect the interest of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
To amend the Market Code of San Mateo, Isabela to allow sub-leasing.

Ordinance No. 2020-815, An Ordinance Amending Section 2A.09.D and Section 3A.02 of Ordinance No. 2011-606 otherwise known as the Market Code of San Mateo, Isabela.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management shall stop the condonation or reduction of real property tax and interest thereon without the corresponding Municipal Ordinance, and enact a Municipal Ordinance in relation thereto, after satisfying the conditions prescribed by Article 276 of the Local Government Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: The LGU of San Mateo, emphasized thru their AAPSI that the condonation or reduction of RPT and interest shall be stopped.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly require sworn statements and ITR supported by the financial statements to serve as legal basis in the computation of business tax as required by the pertinent Rules and Regulations.
observations.managementAction:
The LGU required  sworn statements and Income Tax Returns supported by financial statements from the businesses in the assessment of taxes.

If none is provided, due to its business size, LGU followed the Presumptive Income Level and to apply just and applicable assessment on business tax.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that management in coordination with the Provincial Environment Monitoring Board of Isabela and concerned barangay officials to:

monitor quarry operations in their respective barangays/localities;

report to the concerned authorities (Provincial Management Monitoring Board) all quarry operators who are extracting quarry materials beyond the volume stated in the hauling slip and outside of the allowed quarry sites; and

require the municipal accountant to validate the correctness of the share that the Municipality is receiving from the Province in accordance with the authorized rates and the reported volume of quarry extractions.
observations.managementAction:
A strict and sound internal control to be formulated and implemented to properly determine the accuracy of our income from tax on sand, gravel and other quarry products.

Reconciliation shall be done to determine the accuracy of the municipal share.

A strict and sound internal control shall be formulated and implemented to properly account the collections at the Pay CR.

Limited funds for regular position in the LGU.

Limited funds for regular position in the LGU.

Management ensured that a strict and sound internal control shall be formulated and implemented to properly account the collections at the Pay CR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management regularly assess the financial performance of the Pasalubong Center by comparing the actual revenue against its expenditures and formulate solutions towards financial self-sufficiency or 100% cost recovery.
observations.managementAction:
Management ensured that the operations and the financial performance of the Pasalubong Center shall be monitored regularly.

Also, management ensured the formulation of measures to increase the revenues and reduce the expenditures.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

observe segregation of duties and designate different employees to man the assessment and collection function, respectively, to promote a sound internal control. Immediately assign personnel other than the Collector to be in-charge in the assessment of slaughter fees. Official Receipts must likewise be supported with Order of Payment to ensure that every slaughter activity is properly assessed and receipted;

assign Collecting Officers who are permanent employees and secure a bond from the Bureau of Treasury; and

ensure that collection function is to be handled by the MTO.
observations.managementAction: A sound internal control was formulated and implemented. The assessment function is being done by the slaughterhouse master and the collection function is being done by a permanent employee of the Municipal Treasury Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management, thru the Sangguniang Bayan, adopt and pass a resolution creating casual positions for various departments/offices purposely for the qualified JOWs.  Further, we recommended that the HRMO be required to evaluate the performance of these JOWs assigned in various departments/offices and determine if some of them would be qualified to be hired as regular employees and/or elevated to casual status.  In this manner, the frequent observation as to these JOWs assigned or designated to perform duties and functions of regular employees could be minimized if not eliminated.
observations.managementAction:
A study was conducted for possible transformation from job order to casual.  Department Heads have submitted their list of positions to the Sangguniang Bayan for inclusion in the plantilla position and was already approved.

Profiling of job order workers has been conducted to assess their performance and qualifications.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the persons liable to settle the remaining balances of audit suspensions and disallowances.
observations.managementAction: There were refunds made to the disallowances and partial submission of documents for the transactions suspended in audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to:

require the Accounting Office to prepare a billing statement to the respective contractors, for the latter to return the under withheld taxes amounting to P173,228.12; otherwise, the said amount will be disallowed in audit;

instruct the Treasury and Accounting Offices’, upon return by the contractor, to remit the same amount to BIR representing the underpayment of taxes; and

require the Accounting Office, for succeeding payments of infrastructure projects, to be cautious in using the correct tax rates to ensure proper computation of the amount of taxes to be withheld in accordance with pertinent Revenue Regulations
observations.managementAction: The amounts were refunded by the contractor.
Management ensured regular remittance to the concerned agencies.

observations.recommendation:
We strongly reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct a physical inventory of all the Municipality’s property and correspondingly prepare and submit to the Audit Team the required inventory report thereon. The discrepancies noted between the inventory report and accounting and property records should be investigated and make the necessary adjustments to reconcile the PPE accounts. Also, Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) shall be prepared to account for all unserviceable and obsolete items previously taken up in the books as assets, so that they may be properly disposed of and dropped from the accounts;
b. OIC - Municipal Accountant to provide a complete Lapsing Schedule to support the PPE balances as well as the provision for Depreciation Expenses and Impairment loss, if any, in the financial statements. Moreover, make certain that complete PPELCs are properly maintained and updated so that proper reconciliation with the property cards and PARs of the GSO can be done; and
c. GSO and the Property Officer to ensure that Property Cards and PARs are completely maintained and updated to facilitate the physical count of all the Municipality’s properties. Also, determine the officials/employees to whom the equipment was issued and issue the corresponding PAR for each property to pinpoint accountability and responsibility. Lastly, assure that PARs are renewed at least once in every three years or every time there is a change in custodian of the property
observations.managementAction: For the year 2019, the Concurrent GSO made an actual physical inventory of Municipality's properties and submitted the report to the Audit Team. However, there were discrepancies noted between the inventory report and accounting and property records for the year 2020. The Municipal Accounting Office had already started the PPELC in proper format and will continuously update for reconciliation with property cards and PARs. The preparation of PPELCs for all properties is ongoing and maintenance of the same only started in year 2016.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office and GSO were in-charge to pack and distribute the constituents amidst the COVID-19 pandemic and typhoon Ulysses. Reiterated in Audit Observation no.1 page no. 56

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a.Municipal Treasurer to immediately provide the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectibles to the Municipal Accounting Office; and
b.OIC-Municipal Accountant to establish SLs for RPT and SET Receivables to support the balances presented in the financial statements and reclassify the previous years’ uncollected balances from non-current to current asset.
observations.managementAction: OIC-Municipal Accountant established SLs for RPT and SET Receivable to support the balances presented in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal General Services Officer and the OIC-Municipal Accountant with the complete description and detailed cost segregation of road components of completed road projects of the Municipality for years prior to 2012 to facilitate the conduct of inventory, reconciliation and proper recognition of the same in the books of accounts;
b. Municipal General Services Officer to conduct inventory of all completed road projects of the Municipality, based on the Municipal Engineering Office report on the complete descriptions and cost segregation of road components, as required under COA Circular No. 2015-008; and
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant upon receipt of the corresponding required reports from the Municipal General Service and the Municipal Engineering Offices make the necessary adjustments to recognize the cost of the previous years’ Local Road Network in order to present the correct balance of the Local Road Networks account in the financial statements
observations.managementAction: The concerned Municipal Offices have been collaborating to determine the details of public infrastructures constructed in previous years specifically the municipal road networks to facilitate the yearly inventory and reporting, preparation of the corresponding complete description and cost segregation and recording in the books of accounts. The GSO Office has conducted the physical count of the existing Road Networks and prepared the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Road Networks for CY 2020 with the list provided by the Municipal Engineer as their reference. Also, continuous coordination with the concerned offices shall be exerted for the completion of information pertaining to the Road Networks for the compliance of the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: The GSO could not conduct inventory of all completed road projects of the Municipality due to the non-submission of the complete descriptions and cost segregation of road components by the Municipal Engineering Office.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies and materials in the appropriate inventory account/s and ensure that deductions/credits to these accounts are based on the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) supported by Requisition Issuance Slips (RIS) prepared by the Pharmacist and the Municipal Health Officer; and
b. Municipal Health Office/Pharmacy Department to maintain stock cards for control and monitoring purposes of inventory items and for purposes of reconciliation with the ledger cards being maintained by the Accounting Office; and
c. Municipal Accountant and Supply/Property Officer to ensure that all donated medical, dental and laboratory supplies and drugs and medicines from other government agencies and private entities are properly received and accounted for.
observations.managementAction: The GSO Officer in her management reply dated April 27, 2021 averred that they will ensure inventory control and monitoring by continually effecting proper inventory receipt and issuance procedures for all supplies and materials. Moreover, she will constantly coordinate with the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that complete documentations for all procurement transactions will be implemented for compliance with the audit recommendations. On the other hand, the OIC-Municipal Accountant stated that deductions in the inventory accounts will be based on the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) starting year 2021. He also stated that with the continuous coordination with the GSO and the Information and Communication Technology Office (ICTO), the development of Inventory System will be pursued. The said system was already on the testing phase. The Municipal Accounting Office stated that they will comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
a. make a detailed review of all the recorded reciprocal accounts and coordinate with the Provincial Accounting Office in order to determine the cause/s of the unreconciled amounts of P1,128,311.31 and P3,811,311.39 between the recorded balances of Due from and Due to LGUs of the Municipality and the Rizal Provincial Government. Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts; and henceforth,
b. exercise due professional care in the recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of the reciprocal accounts be the same at all times.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation between the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Provincial Accountant occurred to determine the cause/s of the unreconciled amounts, however differences were still noted for the CY 2020.
observations.reason: Reconciliation is still on-going between the Municipal Accountant and the Provincial Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to hasten the submission of the request for the authority to write-off the dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit, duly supported with complete supporting documents pursuant to Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016. Moreover, restore the dormant Due from Other Funds account amounting to P390,092.58 that were dropped off without proper approval from the COA.
observations.managementAction: Letters of Request No. SMR-WOFF-2019-1 to 4 all dated January 6, 2020 were submitted to the Audit Team on January 7, 2020 however upon review of the request, the supporting documents were incomplete. On the other hand, as of May 25, 2021, the OIC-Municipal Accountant has prepared JEV No. 300-2021-04-01 dated April 21, 2021 to restore the Due From Other Funds account amounting to R329,051.60.
observations.reason: The submitted request for authority to write-off lacked the pertinent supporting documents. As a result, the request could not be properly evaluated by the Audit Team. The OIC-Municipal Accountant prepared JEV to restore part of the dropped account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the above subject accounts and remit whatever the balance due to the agencies concerned to avoid incurrence of penalties, surcharges and inconveniences to the employees concerned. Strictly remit in full the withheld taxes, premium contributions and loan amortizations to the respective government agencies in accordance with the cited regulations;
b. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to hasten, reconcile and trace the amount of P122,548.14 with the concerned government agency so that appropriate disposition on the said balances can be undertaken; and
c. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to make a detailed review of the Due to BIR account of SMMC in order to determine the cause/s of the abnormal balance and thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to present the correct balance of this account
observations.managementAction: As of January 2021, 96 percent of the outstanding balance of Due to BIR account was remitted. Moreover, the Municipal Accounting Office will make a detailed review of the noted abnormal account balance amounting to P48,975.35 of the Due to BIR of SMMC and effect the necessary adjustment to present the correct balance. Also, the Accounting Office will exert their best effort to hasten, reconcile and trace the noted non-moving accounts of Due to GSIS account amounting to P122,548.14 and ensure that appropriate action will be taken. Management also assured that the Accounting office and the Treasury office will make a reconciliation of the noted accounts and will further review and strictly remit in full the amount to the respective government agencies to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of the delinquent real property taxes by initiating tax campaign efforts to collect these delinquent taxes by offering incentives for full payment of tax dues and impose necessary remedies enumerated in Section 256 to 260 of RA no. 7160 or the Local Government Code so that these collections may be utilized for the Municipality’s development programs and projects.
observations.managementAction: Tax campaign efforts were exerted by concerned agencies to collect these delinquent taxes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Government Head, HRMO to consider filling the vacant plantilla positions for more effective administration of the concerned municipal offices and efficient delivery of basic services through hiring qualified applicants and promoting deserving non-permanent personnel to gain the CSC qualifications and to promote deserving personnel.
observations.managementAction: Recruitment efforts were made by Management by posting the vacant plantilla positions online through various platforms and in various conspicuous places. Moreover, CSC exam review classes were conducted by Management for their personnel to gain the CSC qualifications.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to evaluate and assess the actual necessity of the unimplemented PPAs and consider re-programming the corresponding funds for PPAs that are viable to implement and responsive to the actual needs of the constituents to ensure the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental outcomes;
b. Municipal Finance Committee to collaborate with the Sanggunian Bayan for the immediate replenishment of the 20% DF amounting to R51,772,505.00 from the general fund proper budget; and
c. Refrain from using 20% DF for purposes other than those provided in DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-01.
observations.managementAction: Due to COVID-19 pandemic, the savings from 20% DF were realigned by the Municipality for the procurement of welfare goods for distribution to their constituents during the community quarantine. From March 17, 2020 up to May 31, 2020, the Municipality was able to distribute relief goods up to six waves or more depending on the COVID cases in the area. Other MOOE for COVID-19 expenses includes packed meals, rapid test kits, beds and other materials for isolation facilities, PPE and disinfectants. The OIC-Municipal Budget Officer averred that the amount realigned for the COVID-19 expenses from 20% DF were not fully utilized since there were some donations in kind received by the Municipality from various private individuals/entities especially during typhoon Ulysses and therefore the Municipality opted not to utilize the remaining balance of the realigned funds for COVID responses.
observations.reason: Due to COVID-19 pandemic and donations in kind received (especially during typhoon Ulysses), some PPAs were not implemented and savings were realigned to COVID-19 related expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management provide proof of demand letter and proof of payment made by the beneficiaries on these dead animals.
observations.managementAction: None. Liability for losses of government property shall be borne by the accountable officers, if found negligent in keeping of said property.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adhere with DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in the preparation of Annual Investment Plan to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund to ensure that all items to be funded are development programs/projects and activities which are compliant with the guidelines.
observations.managementAction: Management stand that the promotion for the ESWM funded by the 2017 EDF was implemented in the last quarter of CY 2017 and the prizes given were obligated during 1st and 2nd quarters of CY 2018.  It also pointed out that as to the nature of expenses, though it is not a major capital expenditure, giving prizes is one way of encouraging their beneficiary to support, improve and promote RA 9003.  Nonetheless, management committed to ensure that the programs and projects chargeable against the 20% DF shall take the nature of capital expenditure.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management in coordination with the Provincial Environment Monitoring Board of Isabela and concerned barangay officials to:

[a] monitor quarry operations in their respective barangays/localities;

[b] report to the concerned authorities (Provincial Management Monitoring Board) all quarry operators who are extracting quarry materials beyond the volume stated in the hauling slip and outside of the allowed quarry sites; and

[c] require the municipal accountant to validate the correctness of the share that the Municipality is receiving from the Province in accordance the authorized rates and the reported volume of quarry extractions.
observations.managementAction: A task force for quarry operations has been organized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan adopt strategies to enforce collection of these accounts.  Send demand letters compelling them to immediately settle their balances.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to those who have a pending obligations/ delinquent rentals to the municipality.  An “Oplan Putol” was undertaken to various concessionaires with unpaid water bills and both the pasalubong and MPPC receivables were collected.

[b]make the necessary adjusting entry/ies to correct the erroneous and misclassification of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. concerned Municipal officials to determine and find solutions as to the cause/s of the continuous inability of the Treasurer to prepare and submit the certified list of taxpayers and the tax collectible from each at the beginning of the year to the Municipal Accountant as a basis of recording the RPT and SET Receivables, so that accurate and reliable amount of receivables can be taken up in the books of accounts and Management will be provided with accurate information needed in its decision making. If necessary, close coordination with the Municipal Assessor should be done by the Municipal Treasurer in order to come up with the required list;
b. Municipal Accountant to establish Subsidiary Ledgers for RPT and SET Receivables and their corresponding Liability accounts in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor with the aid of their computerized records to safeguard against inaccurate information and improvement of the system for control purposes; and
c. Municipal Accountant to set-up the RPT and SET Receivables and their corresponding Liability accounts in accordance with Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and make the necessary adjustments in the Municipality’s book to present the correct balance of the Deferred RPT and SET and Other Deferred Credits accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that the Municipality’s existing program wherein the RPT and SET Receivables due from each barangay could be accessed. Accordingly, the Treasury Collection Information System or Real Property Tax Information System will be improved so that the list of all delinquent tax payers and the amount due to them will be readily available.
observations.reason: The program or database given to the Audit Team was still incomplete and provided insufficient details.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to make a detailed review of all the recorded reciprocal accounts and coordinate with the Provincial Accounting Office in order to determine the cause/s of the unreconciled amounts. Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/ corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts. Henceforth, exercise due professional care in the recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of the reciprocal accounts at all times be the same.

observations.recommendation:

b. concerned Municipal officials to maximize the utilization of the said fund to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality as envisioned.
c. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer to revert the unappropriated surplus the unobligated balance of the appropriation for MOOE.

We recommended management to initiate legal remedies, if warranted for the recovery of unreturned money amounting to ₱155,678.49 which is due to Philhealth Office.

We recommended the following to management:
[a] determine the purpose of all purchases of drugs, medicines, medical and dental laboratory supplies prior to its recognition in the books of accounts to ensure correct recording of transactions; and
[b] make the necessary adjusting entry/ies to correct the erroneous and misclassification of accounts.

We recommended that management:
[a] require the GSO to maintain stock cards and kept up-to-date for each item particularly drugs and medicines as well as Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies; and
[b] require the Municipal Accounting Office to record all purchases to its corresponding inventory account classification and to adjust this account for whatever consumption on the basis of Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI), duly supported with the original copy of Requisition Issue Slip (RIS).

We recommended the following:
[a] Conduct an inventory of all Public Infrastructure assets under the LGU’s jurisdiction using the format prescribed under COA Circular No. No. 2015-008.  Ensure that all the fields in the form be properly filled up and furnish the Audit Team a copy as basis in validating the correctness of such accounts;
[b] Determine all Public Infrastructure assets transferred to RPI which are not yet fully depreciated or impaired to be restored and recorded in the books as part of PPE accounts; and
[c] Ensure compliance with accounting and reporting guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2015-008 by directing the key officials to perform the duties and responsibilities assigned to them.
We recommended that management provide proof of demand letter and proof of payment made by the beneficiaries on these dead animals.

We recommended that the Accountant draw journal entry voucher reclassifying liability account to government equity account of the unutilized balance of ₱366,898.25 for fair presentation of the financial statements. Likewise, succeeding shares of the LGU from tobacco excise taxes shall be recognized in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs.

We recommended that management adhere with DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in the preparation of Annual Investment Plan to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund to ensure that all items to be funded are development programs/projects and activities which are compliant with the guidelines.

We recommended that these noted deficiencies be refunded to the coffers of the municipal government. Likewise, management ensures that all works to be undertaken should be in accordance with the POW.  Avoid inclusion of unnecessary project cost which is not part of the item of work to be bid.

We recommended that management in coordination with the Provincial Environment Monitoring Board of Isabela and concerned barangay officials to:
[a] monitor quarry operations in their respective barangays/localities;
[b] report to the concerned authorities (Provincial Management Monitoring Board) all quarry operators who are extracting quarry materials beyond the volume stated in the hauling slip and outside of the allowed quarry sites; and
[c] require the municipal accountant to validate the correctness of the share that the Municipality is receiving from the Province in accordance the authorized rates and the reported volume of quarry extractions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan adopt strategies to enforce collection of these accounts.  Send demand letters compelling them to immediately settle their balances.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the persons liable to settle the audit disallowances.

direct the:

b. Municipal Accountant to establish Subsidiary Ledgers for RPT and SET
Receivables and their corresponding Liability accounts in coordination with the

system for control purposes; and
c. Municipal Accountant to set-up the RPT and SET Receivables and their
corresponding Liability accounts in accordance with Section 20 of the MNGAS
for LGUs, Volume I and make the necessary adjustments in the Municipality’s
book to present the correct balance of the Deferred RPT and SET and Other
Deferred Credits accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly with the provisions of Section 51 of the
MNGAS for LGUs, on the proper recording and reporting of all regular
purchases of supplies including the medical, dental and laboratory supplies
and drugs and medicines supplies as inventory account and maintenance of
subsidiary ledger cards;

for control and monitoring purposes;

corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the

a. demand immediate settlement of all unliquidated cash advances from officials

advances should not be granted additional cash advance, until the previous

a] 	adhere to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2013-004 and 2016-003 on the specifications for the signboards, tarpaulins to be posted where the projects/programs and activities of the local government unit in order to fully adopt the full disclosure policy governing public interest;
b] 	furnish the Audit Team with a list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year with complete details as stated in the Circular; and
c] 	refrain from incurring expenses in violation of the afore-cited Circular as this shall be disallowed in audit and subject to administrative disciplinary action provided under penalty clause of the same COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: Management take actions as per your recommendations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the SB, in the succeeding result of SP review action in the municipality’s annual budget, be required to [a] Review the SP review action and determine the concerned offices compliance with the recommendations; [b] monitor the concerned offices compliance with the recommendations; and [c] Inform the SP office of the actions taken on their recommendation.
observations.managementAction: The SB thru their secretariat complied with audit  recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the following remedial measures be undertaken to regulate the giving of financial assistance:

a] 	Formulate a policy for the granting of financial assistance to its constituents taking into consideration the need and budget for the year.  Beneficiaries should be properly screened giving priority to the less privileged constituents. 
b] 	Coordinate with the social welfare department to assess requests for medical/burial assistance by way of social case study reports so that only deserving indigents are assisted and served.
c] 	The financial assistance should be set at an amount that will ease the financial burden of the beneficiaries; and
d] 	Financial assistance granted should be made by check payable to beneficiaries and duly acknowledged by recipients.
e] 	Request for assistance for construction materials must be coursed through the General Service Office of the Municipality so that the usual regulation or procurement be applied and delivered directly to requesting barangays, organizations or whoever requested them.
f] 	Management should be vigilant in ensuring that financial assistance shall be properly receipted and documented and stop the practice of reimbursement of cash assistance requested from elective officials.
observations.managementAction: Through the coordination with Municipal Health Officer and Social Welfare Department, the management developed a policy for the granting of financial assistance and it is now being implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management formulate a policy guideline that sets appropriate rates taking into account their qualifications and responsibilities to be equally applied to these job order employees in accordance with existing rules.
observations.managementAction: A matrix rates was developed and a policy on appraising the services of the Job Order is taking in place.
Management stated that they will be working hand in hand to address these audit observations. Furthermore, the IIRUP will be prepared to effect the disposal of the identified unserviceable items.
observations.reason: The Municipality was not able to conduct a complete physical inventory of some property. Some complete and proper property in the report have no costing making it difficult to reconcile. Reiterated in audit observation no. 1 of, page no. 57

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Municipal Treasury and Accounting Offices to conduct immediate reconciliation of their respective records in order to determine the causes of the discrepancy amounting to P214,561.66 between the General Ledger and Cashbook balances of Cash in Bank accounts to come up with an accurate and reliable financial data;
b. Municipal Accountant to analyze and determine the effects of valid identified reconciling items on the Cash in Bank account’s balances and make the necessary upon validation adjusting/correcting entries; and
c. Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements.
observations.managementAction: Management will analyze the reconciling items as well as the discrepancies noted to effect proper reconciliation. They will also comply with the other audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Reconciliations were being made between the two offices, however minimal discrepancy was still noted by the Audit Team upon validation.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant:
a. to demand immediate settlement of all unliquidated cash advances from officials and employees concerned, pursuant to above cited regulations. Further, ensure that the officers and employees with outstanding or unliquidated cash advances should not be granted additional cash advance, until the previous ones are settled, to preclude accumulation of unliquidated cash advances. Henceforth, ensure that all regulations pertaining to cash advances should be strictly enforced; and
b. to exert efforts to compel the 12 officials/employees that are no longer connected or resigned from the Municipality to liquidate their outstanding cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.
observations.reason: There was improvement in the management of cash advances. However, the cash advances in process connected with officials/employees no longer connected with the LGU were still outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated that the Local Chief Executive with the concurrence of the Municipal Council, hasten the submission of the request of the Municipal Accountant for the authority to write-off the dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit, duly supported with the list of available records pertaining to the accounts, certification as to the extent of validation made on the accounts, reasons why the records/documents/schedules could not be located and the number of years the accounts have been dormant.
observations.managementAction: Management will exert efforts to comply with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: The accounting office reviewed the details of other dormant accounts and revealed that the existence of other dormant accounts were due to previous erroneous entries. Adjusting entries were made to correct the errors. However, there were still dormant accounts due for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant make a detailed review of the accounts and determine the causes of the irregular balances and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of these accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Irregular balances were decreased as compared to prior years. Some irregular balances still remained as account balances were forwarded from previous years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Government Head, HRMO to:
a. consider filling up the vacant plantilla positions which are vital in the delivery of the basic services by hiring qualified applicants or by promoting non-permanent deserving personnel to permanent positions, in which, it would lessen the need to hire job order/contractual workers; and
b. evaluate the performance of Casual/Job Order employees to open opportunity to qualified employees for possible appointment to permanent regular positions to recognize their exemplary performance and commitment in the government service.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that they were exerting efforts to fill up the vacant plantilla positions. They will ensure that vacancies are open to all qualified men and women provided that they meet the minimum requirements for the said positions.
observations.reason: Efforts were exerted to promote or recruit for vacant positions, however several vacant positions were still present as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Award Committee conduct mandatory review to determine reasons for failed biddings and revised the terms, conditions, and specifications, including cost estimates, if necessary.
observations.managementAction:
The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) conducted the mandatory review and evaluation of the terms, conditions, and specifications in the Bidding Documents, including its cost estimates, for every failed public bidding in compliance with the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA 9184.

For every failed bidding, the BAC immediately conducted mandatory review, as mandated by the Revised IRR, in order to determine the reason/s for the failed biddings. As proof, BAC Resolutions specifically and explicitly mentioned the reason/s for the immediately conducted failed bidding, such as, no response to the invitation, no bidder purchased bid documents and no submitted bid proposals before the deadline. Etc.

Further, the BAC through its Resolution/s, likewise recommended the Municipal Mayor the review and reposting of the project after the mandatory review by the BAC.

After the second failed bidding, another Resolution is passed by the BAC declaring as such and the resort to negotiated procurement, as provided for in Section 53.1 of the Revised IRR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that payment of wages for job order employees performing administrative functions be paid through LBP ATM Payroll system in the same manner that regular employees are being paid.
observations.managementAction:
The recommendation that payment of wages for job order employees performing administrative functions to be paid through LBP ATM Payroll system is not feasible because they are not considered government employees, the nature of their stay is temporary and the duration of their appointment is good only for six months, renewable depending on the necessity of their assigned task.

As stated in the AOM, the grant and liquidation of cash advance for the wages of Job Contract and Contract of Service was properly handled by the concerned officials, hence continuation of payment is requested. Rest assured that the LGU will maintain a strong internal control in all transaction pertaining to payments of wages.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the provisions of Section 45 of R.A. 10717. Require the employees with existing obligations from private institutions to settle their obligations personally.
observations.managementAction:
The LGU San Mateo, Isabela ceased to enter into a Memorandum of Agreement with non-government financial institution who engaged in lending for the benefit of government.

On the part of the Municipal Accountant as well as the Municipal Treasurer and HRMO, assured that the net take home pay of ₽4,000.00 for each employee be the primordial consideration before allowing each employee to apply a loan and services from association/cooperative funds organized and managed by government employees and to government financial institution upon payment of their loan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the contract entered into by the Municipal Officials in behalf of the LGU San Mateo be rescinded as this is contrary to existing laws. Moreover, we recommended to the Municipal Officials to be prudent in entering into contract in behalf of the LGU San Mateo to avoid further damage and losses to the government.
observations.managementAction: It noted that the Memorandum of Agreement entered into between the LGU Municipal Officials and the Rural Bank of San Mateo will be the last.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU Officials comply with the above stated Circular in the submission of Contracts to COA. And that concerned offices should obtain and keep file for their reference and basis for actions being taken. Additionally, recommended that reconsider all contracts entered into that pertain to the salary deductions in payrolls to comply with the net take home pay of ₽4,000.00 for each employee.
observations.managementAction: Copies of Memorandum of Agreement with SMMEA and SMMEMPCI have been provided to the Commission on Audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advance.
observations.managementAction: The finance committee and other officials are not allowing for cash advance to elected officials other than for travelling purposes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant ensure the submission of a detailed list of its creditors with particularity at least as to the date the obligation was incurred, the nature of the transaction, the OBR, JEV, DV and check numbers and corresponding dates to disclose in a separate schedule unclaimed credits for ten (10) years or more for reversion of balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant provided schedules of those deposits intended for specific purpose as indicated in the report coming from various individual or groups, to determine the existence of creditor for the year ended 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management demand settlement of receivables from Ms. De Guzman. Also, communicate with the former Municipal Accountant to obtain information on the nature, names, and other records pertaining to those unidentified debtors, if warranted, require immediate settlements.

We further recommended reconciliation of records with the Municipal Assessor’s Office to resolve the recorded balance of problem Taxable assessments RPT.
observations.managementAction:
LGU San Mateo demand the settlement of receivables from Ms. Vedette De Guzman wherein effective March 2018, management will start to deduct from her salary until its full settlement.

A reconciliation of records with the Municipal Assessor’s Office to resolve the recorded balance of problem Taxable assessments RPT is undertaken and supporting documents have been provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the General Services Officer to follow up the transfer receipts of the vehicles to the PGI and other documents necessary for the transfer of ownership at the Land Transportation Office; and the Municipal Accountant to record the vehicles in the books of agency at fair market value considering other adjustments related to the counterpart of the LGU.
observations.managementAction:
The Legislative body of San Mateo drafted a Resolution No. 2017-128 and 2017-129 requesting the transfer of ownership through a donation of Hyundai Grand Starex GL TCT and Toyota UV Starex with plate number SJP 812 respectively to the local government unit of San Mateo dated August 8, 2017.

Last January 29, 2018 the Municipal General Service Officer of LGU San Mateo received the copy of Acknowledgement receipt for the Equipment for the said vehicles and has been recorded as property by the municipality of San Mateo as of this writing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the hiring of job orders and contract of services be made on the necessity of the services to be rendered to maximize the utilization of the scarce resources of the government.

Furthermore, the 28% of the MOOE which was utilized for payment of Other Professional Services should be given attention since there are several objects of expenditures in the MOOE which are all necessary for rendering public service.

We also recommended that the full amount of RATA granted to Mr. Magno in CY 2016, and thereafter be refunded in favor of the LGU, since no appropriation under Personal Services was passed for this purpose; including the amount paid to Ms. Vicente in CY 2016.
observations.managementAction: While it is true that there are programs and projects downloaded to LGU San Mateo, the implementation and operation is the responsibility of said LGU. So, there is necessity to hire focal persons on said programs and projects to facilitate coordination and supervision with the different agencies and implementation. Ms. Vicente refunded the amount while Mr. Henry Magno filed an appeal to the Regional Director of Commission on Audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that disbursement of government funds be governed by the fundamental principles enumerated in the Section 305 of the 1991 Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: The LGU San Mateo is trying to comply with your recommendation on granting financial assistance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the full amount of communication expense paid to Mr. Cristobal in CY 2016 and thereafter, be refunded to the LGU; otherwise, those officials who authorized this disbursement are also held liable for the refund.
observations.managementAction: Notice of Disallowance was issued and Mr. Reynaldo Cristobal refunded the total amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the prescribed period of the submission of accounts and reports.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement vouchers, cash receipts vouchers, liquidation reports, and its journals for the year 2017 are submitted to the Commission on Audit.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Local Chief Executive give utmost importance on presenting in the financial statements PPE account balances that are valid and reliable by: a) directing the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the Agency’s properties, prepare and submit the required report thereon. Any discrepancies between inventory report and the accounting and property records should be investigated and make the necessary adjustments to reconcile those PPE records; b) Accounting Office maintain PPE ledger cards for all PPE accounts and keep the balances in the PPE ledgers and general ledgers reconciled at all times; c) General Services Officer to maintain a complete and updated property records to counter check the correctness of the accounting records; and d) Municipal Accountant to provide a complete Lapsing Schedule to support the PPE balances in the financial statements as well as the provision for Depreciation Expense.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The Concurrent Municipal General Services Officer committed to continue exerting efforts to verify the correctness of the property records to be able to generate the updated and accurate inventory records, and assured that continued thrust to generate a comprehensive and accurate RPCPPE, complete physical counting of PPE items shall be effected for CY 2018 and pertinent reports shall be submitted for compliance. Also, the Municipal Accountant disclosed that unaccounted P6,713,853.60 assets prior to CYs 2002 to 2017 can be considered as fully depreciated in the SEF books.
observations.reason:
Inventory of Property was not complete
The prescribed format of PPE ledger card was not used.
Property cards only consist of latest acquisitions only from CY 2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Accounting Office to conduct immediate reconciliation of their records in order to determine the causes of discrepancy amounting to P 5,824,694.76 between the General Ledger and Cashbook balances of the Cash in Bank account to come up with an accurate and reliable financial data. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare immediately the necessary adjustments to avoid accumulation thereof and to present the correct balance of the cash in bank accounts in the financial statements. Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to regularly submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant promised to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Reconciliation process is continuous. Net discrepancy still noted as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous years’ audit recommendation for the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to expedite the submission of the request for write-off of the dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit, duly supported with the list of available records pertaining to the accounts, proof of efforts exerted to collect the receivables, reasons why the records/documents could not be located and the number of years the accounts have been dormant pursuant to the above regulation. Also, revert to Government Equity, with the concurrence of the Sanggunian Bayan recorded obligations without valid documentation or no actual claims or those accounts which have been outstanding for more than two (2) years.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant averred that the accounts could no longer be retrieved, however, a formal request shall be submitted to COA together with the complete documentation and all the exerted efforts done by the Municipality in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: No request submitted to COA (Reiterated in Observation no. 7 of this AAR, page no. 79 )

observations.recommendation: We strongly recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to determine and verify the cause/s of the abnormal balances of the accounts mentioned above and effect the necessary reclassification or adjustment in the books of accounts to arrive at the correct balances of these accounts. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the financial transactions of the Municipality are properly classified and recorded in the books.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant committed to comply with the audit recommendations, in year 2018.
observations.reason: No action taken by the Municipality. (Reiterated in Observation no. 8 of this AAR, page no. 81 )

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant: a. to demand immediate settlement of all unliquidated cash advances from officials and employees concerned, pursuant to above cited regulations. If warranted, withhold the salaries of those who persistently fail or refuse to comply despite repeated demand for liquidation and/or file appropriate administrative and criminal charges against them to compel liquidation. Further, ensure that the officers and employees with outstanding or unliquidated cash advances should not be granted additional cash advance, until the previous ones are settled, to preclude accumulation of unliquidated cash advances. Henceforth, ensure that all regulations pertaining to cash advances should be strictly enforced; and b. to exert efforts to compel the 12 officials/ employees that are no longer connected or resigned from the Municipal Government to liquidate their outstanding cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
Management exerted efforts in requiring the concerned officials and employees to liquidated the cash advances as evident to the decrease in the Advances. Management noted the audit observation and committed to comply with the recommendations.
observations.reason: Still have unliqudated cash advances at the end of the year; Most of the outstanding cash advances pertains to officers and employees that are no longer connected with the Municipality. Not strictly enforced since there exist unliquidated cash advances at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Management should determine and find solutions as to the cause/s of the continuous failure of the Treasurer to prepare and submit the certified list of taxpayers and the tax collectible from each at the beginning of the year to the Municipal Accountant as a basis of recording the RPT/SET Receivables, so that accurate and reliable amount of receivables can be taken up in the books of accounts and Management will be provided with accurate information needed in its decision making. If necessary, close coordination with the Municipal Assessor should be done by the Municipal Treasurer in order to come up with the required list. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to establish Subsidiary Ledgers for RPT/SET Receivables and its corresponding Liability Accounts in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor with the aid of their computerized records to safeguard against inaccurate information and improvement of the system for control purposes.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant submitted the improvised Certified List of all Real Property maintained by the Assessment Office presenting the assessed value of the Real Property Units (RPUs).
observations.reason: Collectibles no provided by the Treasurer’s Office. Prior years’ practice of setting up RPT receivables were used.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the cash advance voucher together with all the required and proper supporting documents strictly within seven days after the release of the check in a sealed envelope to the Audit Team for post audit. Also, we recommended that the Management justify the urgency or the need for allocating Confidential Fund in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant committed to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason:
Justification was complied
The Municipality complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials concerned to submit the: (a) duly certified correct and approved summarized reports of individual time records of job order employees, indicating the total number of days of service rendered by employees per cut-off; and (b) the accomplishment report of the job order employees duly approved by their respective immediate supervisor. Also, we strongly reiterated that the Municipality stop the practice of processing, approving and paying claims with incomplete documentations. And follow the required stipulations on the job order contracts/contract of service under the CSC regulations.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
Audit Fully complied.
The recommendation complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to promptly remit the unexpended balances of fund transfers to the source agency or Bureau of Treasury as required under Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The unexpended balances of fund transfers were remitted to the source agency as required under Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned Municipal Officials to immediately utilize the amount of P 5,950,000.00 for its intended purposes to ensure that the projects would benefit its constituents. Otherwise, the funds must be returned to the National Government in order that it may be made available for other priority programs or projects of said government agencies.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The Municipality utilized the amount of P 5,950,000.00 for the intended beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit the barangay shares from real property tax collections within five days after the end of each quarter so that such funds could be immediately used in the delivery of services to their constituents.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The shares were remitted within five days after the end of each quarter as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to turn over promptly the disbursement and collection report together with the supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts to the Auditor not later than the tenth (10) day of the ensuing month in compliance with existing regulations and to facilitate the timely audit of the same. Consequently, deficiencies/errors as well as irregularities if any, can be detected immediately enabling the concerned municipal officials to take appropriate action without delay. This will also enable the Audit Team to prepare and submit the report required by stakeholders and government agencies as basis of their decision making.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The Municipality’s accounts were submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive impose compliance with the provisions of RA No. 656, as amended, to insure all properties of the Municipality with the Property Insurance Fund of GSIS, so that in case of loss or destruction of the same, the Municipality could recover the cost of these assets. Moreover, see to it that sufficient funds for the purpose are included in the annual budget of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The Municipality complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC Secretariat and the Procurement Officer to submit copies of Contracts/ Purchase Orders together with all the documents to the Audit Team within the prescribed period, to facilitate the review and evaluation, so that any defects/deficiencies found could be immediately corrected and promptly communicated to the Management. We further, recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Property/Supply Officer to furnish the Audit Team copies of Acceptance and Inspection Reports within 24 hours after acceptance of all consumable deliveries and/or soon after the inspection and acceptance by the LGU personnel concerned so that a timely inspection can be made by the COA representative to ensure that deliveries of goods and services were in accordance with specifications.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The Municipality complied with the recommendation. Perfected Contracts/POs were submitted within five days to COA.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts for cemetery, slaughterhouse and loans in the General Fund, including the maintenance of complete subsidiary ledgers and preparation of the financial statements for these accounts in order to monitor their financial position and efficiency of their operations. Determine also the cause/s of his continued failure to prepare and maintain the required special accounts and find solutions thereto.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The Municipality complied. Special accounts maintained in the Economic Enterprise of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Management maximize the utilization of the 20% DF so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented as planned for the benefit of its constituents. Moreover, we recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the Chief of the Municipal Planning Development Council to adopt an attainable and realistic Plan and Program for 20% Development Fund and a year-end status report on the physical accomplishments viz-a-viz fund utilization be prepared and submitted by the Municipal Engineer’s Office and the Municipal Budget Office for proper evaluation and monitoring of the Municipality’s implemented development projects.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The utilization of the 20% DF was not optimize. The Municipality admitted that the AIP of the Municipality provided insufficient data for the 20% Development Fund and committed to consider the issues tackled on the FY 2019 budget preparation.
observations.reason: Not Still exist year- balances in the 20% DF (Reiterated in Observation no. 16 of this AAR, page no. 96)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct: a) The Local School Board to prioritize expenditures enumerated in RA 5447 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998 and discontinue charging against the SEF expenditures not in conformity with said regulations. Henceforth, prioritization of expenses chargeable to SEF must be strictly enforced; and b) The OIC-Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Local School Board to ensure the maximum and efficient utilization of the SEF for the priority programs and projects pertaining to the operations and maintenance of the public schools within the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The purchases charged to SEF for CY 2017 were contrary to the DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017. The Municipality stated that the use of SEF for the purchase of mobile software can be attributed as means of legitimate usage allowed under the DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017 and the procurement was incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan, Annual Budget and was duly approved by the Local School Board.
observations.reason: Not The proper utilization of SEF was not strictly observed. (Reiterated in Observation no. 12 of this AAR, page no. 89 )

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct: a) The Local School Board to charge the procurement of Computer Software and Office Equipment to the present funds available in the Department of Education instead of charging the payments to SEF as provided in Republic Act No. 5447 and discontinue charging the expenditures against the SEF which is not in conformity with said regulations. Henceforth, prioritization of expenses chargeable to SEF must be strictly enforced; b) The Municipal Budget Officer in coordination with the Local School Board to ensure the maximum and efficient utilization of the SEF for the priority programs and projects pertaining to the operations and maintenance of the public schools within the locality; and c) The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to stop charging to SEF expenditures not in conformity with the DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
According to the Municipal Accountant the use of SEF for the purchase of mobile software can be attributed as means of legitimate usage allowed under the DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017 and the procurement was incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan, Annual Budget and was duly approved by the Local School Board.
observations.reason: Not The Municipal Budget still charged expenditures not in conformity with the DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) the Local Chief Executive establish and strengthen the required GFPS of the Municipality through local issuances, policies and directives to support gender mainstreaming within their localities; b) the targets and accomplishments for GAD activities shall be quantified to facilitate assessment of the Municipality’s attainment of its targets; c) the discrepancies of the GAD budgets and activities appearing in the GAD Plan and GAD Accomplishment Report should be verified and corrected; and d) the provisions of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 should be strictly conformed with on the guidelines for the preparation of Annual GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report. (Note: This appears to be a misplaced recommendation in the source; intended recommendation was to plan and utilize unexpended LDRRMF balances.)
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant upon receipt of the audit observation, immediately exerted effort by coordinating with the Office of the Provincial Accountant, requesting for a copy of the ledgers so reconciliation can be effected.
observations.reason: Some of the MDRRMP programs/projects were integrated in the AIP but not specifically identified in the MDRRM Plan. (Reiterated in Observation no. 18 of this AAR, page no. 104)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the: a. Municipal Budget Officer to allocate at least five percent of the total budget appropriations for the implementation of GAD Plan; b. GAD Focal Person of the Municipality to secure the review and endorsement of the DILG Provincial Office on the GPB disclosing the Agency’s programs, projects and activities designed to address the gender concerns of its constituents and to ensure the efficient utilization of the resources for the attainment of GAD objectives; c. GAD Focal Person to ensure that the targets and accomplishments for GAD activities are quantified to facilitate assessment of the Municipality’s attainment of its targets; d. GAD Focal Person to establish the details of the discrepancies between the GAD budgets and activities appearing in the GAD Plan and GAD Accomplishment Report should be verified and corrected. Likewise, the amount and activities indicated in the GAD Plan and Budget, Fund Utilization Report and Accomplishment Report should be consistent; and e. Municipal GAD Council to strictly conform with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 2013-01, as amended by JMC No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016, on the guidelines for the preparation of Annual GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report that geared towards development of the Municipality's programs, projects and activities designed to address the gender responsive governance, protect and fulfill women’s human rights, promote women’s economic empowerment and gender concerns of its constituents and ensure the efficient utilization of the resources for the attainment of GAD objectives.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The GAD annual budget was rectified in CY 2017 and the required five percent of the total annual budget appropriations was already complied within the year, but reminded the Municipality on the scheduled review and endorsement of the GPB to the DILG Provincial Office of Rizal before the budget preparation.
observations.reason: The provisions of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 on the guidelines for the preparation of Annual GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report were not strictly followed previously but actions were taken; targets and accomplishments were not quantified previously.
