observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entry to record the appropriate account in accordance with the PPSAS Chart of Accounts. It is further recommended that future transactions be analyzed and classified as to the prescribed accounts in COA Circular 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 in order to avoid misstatements and to fairly present the balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Journal Entry Voucher has been drafted this 2016 to reclassify such items indicated in the AOM in conformance with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Officer initiate the necessary procedures for immediate disposal of these unserviceable properties in order to derive benefits from its sale.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer undertaken the disposal of unserviceable properties with the exemption of recyclable items and submitted to the Municipal Accountant the Report of Disposal and been recorded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust to the appropriate account the items identified in the attached schedule. We also recommended that the GSO replace the Acknowledgment Receipt of Equipment issued for this purpose with Inventory Custodian Slip.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer has replaced the issued Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment with those identified items with Inventory Custodian slip. Also, she submitted the summary together with the attached copy where the Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustment debiting an inventory account or expense if determined as unserviceable and crediting the Property Account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advance.
observations.managementAction: There were no elected official who granted cash advance other than travel after your recommendation. Relative thereto, a qualified special disbursing officer bonded under the Bureau of Treasury was appointed. However the Office of the Accountant failed to impose the liquidation of Outstanding Cash Advances prior to the approval of the new ones. However, a demand has been made to MSWDO and a liquidation report has already been submitted as of this writing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management enforce what was stipulated in the contract to show to the lessees not to take lightly on their obligations in using the market stalls and that this would also be an opportunity to those who are willing and responsible interested parties to use the property.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has provided a report on status of market stall rental as of February 2016 for reference and updated her collection per clients. Majority of the stallholders paid and updated their account during the licensing period. For the accounts that remain unpaid as of this date, a demand letters had already been sent to delinquent stallholders and stallholders with long overdue account were referred to Office of Municipal Legal Officer for appropriate action.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management restore to the LDRRMF the total amount of P8,634,754.14 and deposit the same to its depository bank intended for the purpose it was created.

Furthermore, we recommended that management submit the documents and reports related to compliance with our audit recommendation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer provided a summary in support to the utilization of LDRRM Fund dated May 25, 2016. Lastly, The Finance Committee is devoted to comply with the laws on the utilization of LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict compliance to the provisions of the Republic Act 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulation on all procurement of LGU San Mateo to promote transparency, competitiveness and accountability in the government.
observations.managementAction: The Local Government Unit of San Mateo immediately responded to the call of its constituents for help by distributing relief goods. In light of the urgency of the need and as an immediate response to the people affected by typhoon, the said goods have not been subjected to bidding as an exception to the regular bidding process per Sec. 53.2 of the IRR of RA 9184. The Management and the finance committee will continue to observe your recommendation on future disbursement of calamity fund as possible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management comply with the submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, as provided in Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, in order to monitor compliance on accounting and reporting of the utilization of LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer submitted a monthly report on Sources and Utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) last January for the month of December.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management refrain from splitting of requisitions to circumvent the procurement law relative to public bidding. We also reiterated our previous recommendation that the GSO and the BAC consolidate the procurement of similar and related items with dates of requisitions in close proximity into one purchase request and subject the same to competitive public bidding pursuant to provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: As per record kept by the BAC Secretariat, there are 104 notices of competitive bidding posted in the PhilGEPS those not include 83 notices for other forms of procurement. This is an indication that we complied with your recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management strictly comply with the Supreme Court decision by remitting immediately to the Bureau of Treasury all the unutilized PDAF balance of P130,116.97.
observations.managementAction: The management returned the Unutilized balance of Priority Development Assistance Fund to the Bureau of Treasury per Official Receipt Number 6583872.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management ensure the programs and projects in the Annual Investment Plan for the 20% Economic Development Fund be consistent with the provisions of the JMC 2011-01. We also recommended that the management stop charging unrelated expenses against the fund, to avoid audit suspensions or disallowances. Additionally, we recommended that the amount of P560,288.00 be restored to the 20% EDF and to be utilized appropriately.
observations.managementAction: The Budget Officer considered the charging of expenses under 20% development fund in line with the provisions of DBM/DILG JMC 2011-01. However, amounts other than the meals for patients and doctors are being identified as part of the project cost for the programs included in the Annual Development Fund. The Municipal Budget Officer will restore the appropriation utilized to pay the patients and doctor’s meal amounting to P236,304.00 to its appropriate funding account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor terminate the contracts of hired consultants who are performing activities related to the regular functions and responsibilities of existing officials and employees. Hiring of consultants should only be made on special projects and in conformity with existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: After your recommendation, no consultants had been hired as of date and assured by the HRMO to resort on such hiring only if it pertain to special projects and in conformity with existing rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management constantly adhere to the requirement of Bureau of Internal Revenue on withholding taxes to all suppliers of the LGU to avoid penalties of non-compliance with BIR rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant withholds taxes on all suppliers transacting to the Local Government Unit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management stop the practice of allowing reimbursements consistent with PD 1445 and to avoid audit disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The management minimized reimbursements as much as possible as per recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management strictly observe the proper charging of expenditures to appropriate funds and to refund the incentives granted to concerned officials amounting P520,000.00.
observations.managementAction: All the incentives granted to concerned officials have been returned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management require immediate refund of all the RATA received by the designated OIC-MPDC in CY 2015 and thereafter as this is considered unnecessary and irregular.
observations.managementAction: Currently in the process of collecting the amount disallowed.
